You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Only 1 cell changed at [63, 6]. That's at the bottom of the board - probably a timer or step counter. The green square didn't move, which means it's at the top boundary of the gray box (rows 38-39).
So the green square can move within the gray box (rows 38-43, cols 8-13). The 2x2 green square can be at positions:
- Rows: 38-39, 39-40, 40-41, 41-42 (4 positions)
- Cols: 8-9, 9-10, 10-11, 11-12, 12-13 (5 positions)
Wait, but the gray box is 6x6 (rows 38-43, cols 8-13). The green square is 2x2. So it can be at:
- Rows: 38-39, 39-40, 40-41, 41-42 (4 positions vertically)
- Cols: 8-9, 9-10, 10-11, 11-12, 12-13 (5 positions horizontally)
Actually wait, the gray box boundary is [[38, 8], [38, 13], [43, 13], [43, 8]]. That's rows 38-43 and cols 8-13, which is a 6x6 area. The green square is 2x2, so it can be at positions:
- Rows: 38-39, 39-40, 40-41, 41-42 (4 positions)
- Cols: 8-9, 9-10, 10-11, 11-12, 12-13 (5 positions)
Hmm, but the green square is INSIDE the gray box. Looking at the ascii:
Row 38: cggNNgg...
Row 39: cggNNgg...
Row 40: cgggggg...
Row 41: cgggggg...
Row 42: cgggggg...
Row 43: cgggggg...
So the gray box is rows 38-43, cols 8-13 (6x6). The green square is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11. The "gg" at cols 8-9 and 12-13 are the gray walls of the box.
Wait, looking more carefully:
Row 38: cggNNggcccccccccccccccccc
Row 39: cggNNggcccccccccccccccccc
The "c" at col 7 is the background. The "gg" at cols 8-9 is the left wall. The "NN" at cols 10-11 is the green square. The "gg" at cols 12-13 is the right wall.
So the gray box is actually:
- Row 38: ggNNgg (cols 8-13)
- Row 39: ggNNgg (cols 8-13)
- Row 40: gggggg (cols 8-13)
- Row 41: gggggg (cols 8-13)
- Row 42: gggggg (cols 8-13)
- Row 43: gggggg (cols 8-13)
So the gray box is 6x6, and the green square is 2x2 inside it. The green square can move within the box, but it seems to be constrained by the gray walls.
Actually, looking at the ascii more carefully:
Row 38: cggNNggcccccccccccccccccc
The "c" is the background (charcoal). The "gg" at cols 8-9 is the left part of the gray box. The "NN" at cols 10-11 is the green square. The "gg" at cols 12-13 is the right part of the gray box.
So the gray box is 6x6 (rows 38-43, cols 8-13), and the green square is 2x2 inside it. The green square can move within the interior of the box.
But wait, the gray box seems to have walls. Let me check the initial state:
Row 38: cggggggcccccccccccccccccc
Row 39: cggggggcccccccccccccccccc
Row 40: cggNNggcccccccccccccccccc
Row 41: cggNNggcccccccccccccccccc
Row 42: cggggggcccccccccccccccccc
Row 43: cggggggcccccccccccccccccc
So initially, the green square was at rows 40-41, cols 10-11. The gray box is 6x6, with the green square in the middle.
Now the green square is at rows 38-39, cols 10-11 (top of the box). Let me try moving it left to see if it can go to the left edge.
